title: rubus hirtus var. chamaebaetus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. chamaebaetus, commonly known as the dwarf or alpine raspberry, is a low-growing, thorny shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. chamaebaetus include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and prevent certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and dried extracts, and are often used to support overall well-being and promote longevity.
